profile.name: Ayush Juneja
profile.specialty: Next.js, Node.js, E-commerce Systems, Admin Dashboards, Mobile Apps, Automation
profile.availability: Open to full-time roles, freelance builds, and product collaborations
profile.intro: Full stack developer with nearly 2 years of professional experience building scalable web and mobile applications, e-commerce platforms, and business-focused digital systems.
profile.shortBio: I build product systems that balance engineering depth, business clarity, and interface quality across web, mobile, commerce, and internal tools.
profile.aboutLead: I am a full stack developer who enjoys building products that need both strong technical foundations and a clean, confident user experience.
profile.story: My work has grown inside startup-style environments where speed matters, but so do reliability, maintainability, and business impact. That has pushed me toward building systems that are not only functional, but genuinely useful for the people operating them every day.
profile.approach: I like working across the full product surface: frontend polish, backend architecture, admin workflows, analytics, integrations, and automation. The goal is usually the same: turn messy operational needs into products that feel simpler, faster, and easier to trust.

workApproach.title: Business-first product thinking
workApproach.description: I try to understand the operational goal behind every feature so the solution helps the business, not just the codebase.

workApproach.title: Full-stack ownership
workApproach.description: I am comfortable moving from interface decisions to backend contracts, data flow, and system behavior without losing continuity.

workApproach.title: Calm, scalable execution
workApproach.description: I prefer systems that are clean to operate, predictable to maintain, and ready to grow with the product.

education.detail: Completed senior secondary schooling in Delhi, India.

education.detail: Computer Science degree focused on software engineering, system design, and scalable digital product development.

projects.title: BookLeaf Author Support & Communication Portal
projects.description: A production-oriented support operations portal built for author communication, ticket lifecycle management, realtime collaboration, and AI-assisted admin workflows.
projects.summary: Built a practical support and communication system for BookLeaf with real-time ticket handling, AI-generated admin assistance, role-based access control, and scalable backend workflows designed around real operational needs.
projects.problem: BookLeaf needed a support workflow that could help the team manage author communication faster, more consistently, and with reduced manual effort. The challenge was not just building a polished demo, but designing practical ticket lifecycle management, admin actions, AI-assisted responses, and realtime communication that reflect real support operations.
projects.solution: Built a full stack author support and communication portal using Next.js, Express.js, MongoDB, Socket.io, and AI-assisted workflows. The system prioritizes clean backend architecture, realtime ticket communication, role-based access control, scalable ticket management, and production-oriented API design. AI response generation was grounded in BookLeaf-specific knowledge, communication guidelines, and operational policy prompts to keep admin assistance contextual and useful.
features: Real-time ticket communication and status updates with Socket.io
AI-assisted draft responses for admin workflows
Role-based access control for superadmin, admin, and author accounts
Scalable ticket lifecycle management and categorisation flows
Production-oriented REST API structure with Swagger documentation
Realtime read synchronization and live support interactions
Practical admin usability built around support operations
impact: Demonstrated how AI can assist support teams without removing human review
Created a realistic operations-focused system rather than a cosmetic assignment demo
Balanced clean engineering, product thinking, and practical workflow reliability under a tight timeline
Established a scalable base for future analytics, SLA tracking, workflow automation, and queue-driven AI processing
workflow: Author creates or updates a support ticket
Admins receive realtime updates and ticket activity
AI drafts contextual support responses using BookLeaf policies and knowledge
Admin reviews, edits, and sends the final response
Ticket status, priority, and category are updated through the lifecycle
Conversations stay synchronized live between authors and support staff

projects.title: Creator Intelligence Platform
projects.description: An AI-powered creator analysis and RAG platform that turns YouTube and Instagram content into a searchable knowledge base with contextual conversations.
projects.summary: Built a full stack creator intelligence platform that extracts creator content, generates vector embeddings, stores knowledge in ChromaDB, and enables grounded AI conversations using Retrieval Augmented Generation.
projects.problem: Brands, agencies, and marketing teams often spend significant time manually reviewing creator content, engagement patterns, and audience performance before making collaboration decisions. The process is slow, repetitive, and difficult to scale across multiple creators.
projects.solution: Built an AI-powered creator intelligence workflow where users submit creator URLs, content and metadata are extracted, transcripts are chunked, embeddings are generated, knowledge is stored in ChromaDB, and OpenAI produces grounded answers using retrieved creator context.
features: YouTube and Instagram creator analysis
Creator metadata extraction and performance insights
Context-aware AI chat for creator-specific conversations
Retrieval Augmented Generation with streaming responses
Semantic vector search using ChromaDB
Transcript processing, chunking, and embedding generation
Creator comparison and knowledge retrieval workflow
impact: Turned creator content into an intelligent and searchable knowledge platform
Reduced manual review effort for collaboration and evaluation workflows
Demonstrated practical implementation of RAG, vector databases, and semantic search
Showcased a production-style Next.js and FastAPI full stack architecture for AI products

projects.title: Miray Fashions Commerce Ecosystem
projects.description: A production-grade fashion commerce operating system built to manage storefront, inventory, logistics, returns, CRM, reporting, marketing, and admin workflows in one unified platform.
projects.summary: Built Miray Fashions as a complete commerce ecosystem rather than a simple online store, covering the full lifecycle of a fashion business from product creation and inventory planning to customer acquisition, order fulfillment, returns, analytics, and customer retention.
projects.role: Sole Full Stack Developer and Technical Lead
projects.problem: Miray Fashions needed more than a conventional e-commerce website. The business required a centralized system capable of handling high product volumes, large order volumes, customer lifecycle management, logistics, returns, exchanges, reporting, marketing operations, and team collaboration without relying on disconnected third-party tools.
projects.solution: Designed and developed a complete fashion commerce operating system with a mobile-first customer storefront, custom admin and order workflows, inventory tracking, COD verification automation, logistics integrations, returns and exchanges management, production planning, CRM insights, content management, and business intelligence dashboards. The platform unified storefront management, logistics, support, reporting, and automation into one tailored system.
features: Mobile-first storefront with product discovery across categories, collections, search, trending products, and new arrivals
Detailed product pages with image galleries, pricing logic, recommendations, reviews, and related products
Customer account system with order tracking, addresses, returns, exchanges, reviews, and wishlist support
Persistent cart, coupons, Razorpay integration, cash on delivery, dynamic shipping calculations, and optimized checkout
Real-time inventory control with size-wise stock tracking, reservations, available inventory, and production requirements
Custom order lifecycle management for pending, processing, packed, shipped, delivered, returned, exchanged, and RTO flows
COD verification automation with customer confirmation, response handling, admin visibility, and auto-cancellation workflows
Direct logistics integrations with Shiprocket, BlueDart, and EShipz APIs for serviceability, shipments, AWB, labels, and tracking
Dedicated returns, exchanges, and refund workflows for both customers and administrators
Customer analytics, review automation, campaign tracking, short links, traffic attribution, and reporting dashboards
Production management, custom CMS, and role-based admin access for products, orders, marketing, media, and reports
impact: Transformed Miray from a standard online store into a complete business operating system
Reduced dependency on fragmented SaaS tools by centralizing operations into one platform
Improved operational efficiency across fulfillment, returns, logistics, support, and reporting workflows
Created a scalable foundation for a fashion brand to manage customer experience and business intelligence together
Demonstrated deep expertise in e-commerce architecture, business automation, logistics integrations, and admin system design
workflow: Customers discover products through categories, collections, search, and featured merchandising
Orders move through checkout, payment, shipping calculation, and confirmation workflows
Inventory updates in real time with size-wise stock, reservations, and production requirements
Admins process orders, generate shipping labels, monitor courier updates, and manage cancellations
Returns, exchanges, refunds, and COD verification are handled through dedicated operational workflows
Business teams use CRM insights, marketing attribution, CMS tools, and reporting dashboards to drive growth

blogs.title: Building Business-Focused Systems That Actually Scale
blogs.excerpt: Why scalable architecture only matters when it also makes day-to-day business workflows easier.

content.heading: Scalability Is a Business Problem First
content.body: Scalable software is not only about handling more traffic. It is about making sure product, operations, and business workflows remain stable as the company grows.

content.heading: Good Systems Reduce Friction
content.body: The best backend and admin systems create fewer repeated tasks, fewer hidden dependencies, and clearer operational visibility.

blogs.title: Why Admin Dashboards Need Product Thinking
blogs.excerpt: Internal tools deserve the same clarity, responsiveness, and UX discipline as customer-facing products.

content.heading: Internal Tools Still Shape Performance
content.body: If a team spends hours inside a dashboard, that interface has a direct impact on output, error rate, and operational speed.

content.heading: Good UX Helps Teams Move Faster
content.body: Well-designed admin systems reduce handoffs, improve confidence, and make business workflows easier to manage.

blogs.title: Automation Without Chaos
blogs.excerpt: Automation works best when it removes repetitive effort without making systems harder to understand.

content.heading: The Goal Is Reliability
content.body: Automation should make processes more dependable, not more fragile. Clear inputs, traceable outputs, and simple monitoring matter.

content.heading: Business Workflows Need Visibility
content.body: Teams trust automation more when they can see what happened, what changed, and what still needs human judgment.
